chapter: Chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

questions.question: What is indigenous education?
questions.answer: Traditional education system that existed in India before British rule.

questions.question: Name one traditional school in India.
Who were the Orientalists?
questions.answer: Scholars who supported Indian languages and classical learning.

questions.question: Who were the Anglicists?
questions.answer: People who supported English education and Western knowledge.

questions.question: Who wrote the Minute on Indian Education?
In which year was Macaulay’s Minute introduced?
What language did Macaulay support for education?
What is Wood’s Despatch?
questions.answer: An education reform document issued in 1854.

questions.question: In which year was Wood’s Despatch issued?
Name one recommendation of Wood’s Despatch.
Which universities were established in 1857?
Who supported modern scientific education in India?
What class emerged due to English education?
What subjects were introduced through Western education?
What happened to traditional education during British rule?
Which ideas spread through Western education?
What movement was influenced by modern education?
Why did the British introduce English education?
questions.answer: To create educated Indians to assist in administration.

questions.question: What kind of education did Orientalists support?
What type of knowledge did Anglicists promote?
What was the main medium of higher education under British rule?
What role did education play in social reform?
questions.answer: It spread awareness about social issues.

questions.question: What ideas encouraged Indians to question colonial rule?
What is colonial education?
questions.answer: Education introduced by the British during colonial rule.

questions.question: What is one long-term impact of colonial education?
questions.answer: Establishment of modern universities in India.
